title: Creamy Saudi Almond Grill
description: A nutty, creamy dish that blends almond flavor with a subtle Middle Eastern spice profile, cooked in a donabe on a yakimono grill for a smoky finish.
instructions: Preheat the yakimono grill to medium-high and lightly oil the donabe to prevent sticking.
In a bowl, combine gluten-free plain flour and almond flour, whisk until smooth.
Gradually stir in almond milk, olive oil, salt, cumin, coriander, and lemon juice until you get a thick batter.
Pour the batter into the preheated donabe, smoothing the surface with a spatula.
Place the donabe on the grill and close the lid to trap heat for 10 minutes.
After 10 minutes, stir the mixture gently to create a creamy, slightly caramelized coating.
Return to the grill for another 5 minutes until the edges are golden and the center is set.
Remove from heat, let rest 3 minutes, then serve topped with chopped parsley and a drizzle of ghee.
